Title: Regulatory network rewiring drives strain-specific lipid accumulation response in Chlorella sorokiniana under nutrient starvation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Consistent upregulation correlates with TAG accumulation and lipid body formation under nutrient stress.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MLDP orthologs were consistently upregulated during TAG-accumulating conditions in both strains. 2. MLDP is described as a key structural component of stress-induced lipid bodies. 3. Its expression correlated with observed lipid body formation and TAG accumulation under N and P stress.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; transcriptional network inference; correlation with TEM-observed lipid bodies and lipid profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Key structural component of lipid droplets; not an enzyme.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to improve TAG storage capacity and lipid droplet formation in microalgae for biofuel production.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA: PRJNA1272067); lipidomics (UHPLC-MS); FAME metabolite profiling (GC-MS)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under N t2/t3 and P-associated TAG accumulation conditions in both strains
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RRA top-ranked TFs in CsDOE1412 interact with MLDP-encoding genes (specific TF names not fully listed in text)

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: phospholipid replacement branch under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Facilitates conversion of diacylglycerol (DAG) into diacylglyceryl trimethylhomoserine (DGTS).
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phosphorus-free membrane lipid replacement during phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under P starvation in both strains; not induced under N
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes DGTS accumulation specifically under phosphate starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BTA1 orthologs were upregulated under P starvation in both strains. 2. DGTS progressively accumulated under P and became a predominant betaine lipid. 3. Lack of induction under N paralleled absence of DGTS accumulation, supporting P-specific function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; ortholog identification; lipid profiling by UHPLC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant for DGTS biosynthesis; treated as one of the selected critical steps
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering phosphorus-starvation membrane remodeling and stress adaptation in microalgae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No EC number available; identified by orthology to Chlamydomonas reinhardtii Bta1.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert DAG away from TAG toward DGTS under P starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cs1228_04g0256 (NF-YC) inferred to control BTA1 in Cs1228

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: de novo fatty acid synthesis initiation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation in DOE1412 under N t2/t3 and P t2 indicates enhanced de novo FA biosynthesis contributing to higher TAG accumulation; unchanged or downregulated in Cs1228.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACCase is the first and rate-limiting step of de novo FA biosynthesis. 2. CsDOE1412 showed upregulation of two ACCase orthologs under TAG-accumulating conditions. 3. Cs1228 ACCase orthologs were unchanged or downregulated, consistent with weaker lipid production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; literature-based pathway assignment; integration with lipid phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target to increase fatty acid precursor supply for TAG production, especially in high-lipid strains.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA: PRJNA1272067); lipidomics; FAME profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ortholog gene IDs not specified in the text excerpt.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in DOE1412 under N t2/t3 and P t2; unchanged/downregulated in Cs1228
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TAG-related TFs inferred to regulate ACCase-encoding genes in both strains

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Kennedy pathway / de novo TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates glycerol-3-phosphate to initiate glycerolipid/TAG assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation under N and P at t2/t3 in both strains correlates with TAG biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT was identified as a key enzyme in TAG biosynthesis. 2. Homolog genes were upregulated mainly under N and P at t2 and t3 in both strains. 3. Expression correlated with TAG biosynthesis and lipid body formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; E2P2 enzyme annotation; network inference; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Selected as a rate-limiting/critical step in de novo TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to enhance de novo TAG assembly in microalgae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as one of the enzyme-coding genes under control of TAG-related TFs in both strains.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated mainly at N/P t2 and t3 in both strains
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TAG-related TFs inferred to regulate GPAT-encoding genes in both strains

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Kennedy pathway / de novo TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation correlates with TAG accumulation under nutrient stress; de novo acyl-CoA-dependent route appears predominant in DOE1412.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGAT homologs were upregulated under N and P mainly at t2 and t3 in both strains. 2. TAG accumulation in DOE1412 appears to rely predominantly on the acyl-CoA-dependent route ending in DGAT. 3. DGAT is one of the regulated critical steps connected to high TAG accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; E2P2 annotation; network inference; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme / terminal determinant in de novo TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for increasing TAG yield; may be especially effective in high-TAG DOE1412-like regulatory backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA: PRJNA1272067); lipidomics; FAME profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cs1228_04g0256 (NF-YC) inferred to control DGAT in Cs1228; CsDOE1412_2202g05533 (GATA) inferred to control DGAT in CsDOE1412
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ortholog gene IDs not specified in the text excerpt.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under N/P mainly at t2 and t3 in both strains

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from phospholipids such as phosphatidylcholine to diacylglycerol to form TAG.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated or unchanged in DOE1412 under either stress; upregulated in Cs1228 in response to N
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to TAG production mainly in Cs1228 under N; minor role in DOE1412.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDAT represents the acyl-CoA-independent route for TAG synthesis. 2. PDAT homologs were upregulated in Cs1228 under N, suggesting stress-specific use of membrane lipids for TAG formation. 3. PDAT homologs were downregulated or unchanged in DOE1412, indicating a lesser role there.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: InterPro domain-based gene identification; RNA-seq differential expression; pathway inference
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Alternative critical step for TAG production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for mobilizing membrane lipids into TAG, especially in low-TAG strains like Cs1228.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified by InterPro domain because E2P2 did not assign EC 2.3.1.158.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TAG-related TFs inferred to regulate PDAT-encoding genes in both strains
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May consume membrane phospholipids during TAG formation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: TAG biosynthesis via membrane lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: alternative acyl donor route from MGDG hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: side-route supplying acyl donors for TAG production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes monogalactosyldiacylglycerol (MGDG), providing acyl donors for TAG production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Two of five predicted homologs upregulated in DOE1412 under N t2/N t3 or P t2/t3; all predicted homologs upregulated in Cs1228 under N and P
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports TAG accumulation through membrane lipid remodeling, with broader induction in Cs1228 and minor contribution in DOE1412.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGD1 participates in the acyl-CoA-independent route by mobilizing MGDG. 2. In DOE1412, only two predicted homologs were upregulated, suggesting minor contribution. 3. In Cs1228, all predicted homologs were upregulated under N and P, consistent with stronger reliance on membrane remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; pathway inference; correlation with decreased MGDG content
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Alternative critical step for TAG production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to channel thylakoid membrane lipids toward TAG, especially in strains relying on remodeling pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Step 11 showed no interaction with TAG-related TFs in either strain, but was regulated by other TFs in Cs1228 according to RRA top-ranked analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No TAG-related TF interaction detected for step 11 in either strain
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May compromise membrane integrity through MGDG turnover.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the oxidative decarboxylation of isocitrate in the TCA cycle.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of IDH in DOE1412 during TAG-accumulating conditions suggests an important control point for carbon allocation toward lipid biosynthesis, whereas it is downregulated in Cs1228.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cs1228_05g03522 was downregulated under N at all timepoints. 2. CsDOE1412_2253g05814 was upregulated at N t2, N t3, and P t2 when TAG accumulation is evident. 3. The authors suggest IDH is an important control point for carbon allocation for lipid biosynthesis in DOE1412.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; literature-based assignment as rate-limiting TCA step; integration with lipid phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for redistributing carbon flux toward lipid accumulation in high-producing strains.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down in Cs1228 under N; up in DOE1412 at N t2/t3 and P t2
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TAG-related TFs inferred only in Cs1228 for step 3

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes CO2 fixation in the Calvin cycle.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with/redirects carbon allocation relative to target-product branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation under N suggests early shutdown of CO2 fixation and redirection of carbon toward respiration and lipid biosynthesis; one DOE1412 ortholog was upregulated under P.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RuBisCO genes were downregulated in N in both strains. 2. This was interpreted as early shutdown of CO2 fixation and redirection of carbon flow toward lipid biosynthesis. 3. One ortholog in DOE1412 (CsDOE1412_2297g12375) was upregulated under P, suggesting an alternative coping mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important node for carbon partitioning strategies when balancing growth versus storage lipid production.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CsDOE1412_2202g05533 (GATA) inferred to control RuBisCO; RRA top-ranked TFs in DOE1412 also interact with RuBisCO genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down under N in both strains; one DOE1412 ortholog up under P
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely reduced photosynthetic carbon fixation and growth when downregulated.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phosphate-starvation adaptation; carbon and energy storage
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phosphate starvation response; phosphate transport/scavenging; lipid remodeling under P stress
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: H+/Pi cotransporters; phosphate transporters; SPX-domain-containing proteins
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates canonical phosphate-starvation effectors in DOE1412 under P stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong induction correlates with stronger P-starvation phenotype, including greater growth inhibition and TAG accumulation in DOE1412.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDOE1412_2296g00942 is the PHR1 ortholog in DOE1412 and was strongly induced, up to ~10-fold, at early and late P timepoints. 2. Its predicted targets include canonical phosphate-starvation effectors such as phosphate transporters and SPX proteins. 3. Its transcriptional circuitry differs from that in Cs1228, indicating regulatory rewiring between strains.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TRN inference; predicted target analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of phosphate-starvation-driven lipid remodeling in DOE1412
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes that unlike higher plants, PHR1 was transcriptionally induced under P in these microalgae.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering improved phosphate-starvation responses and associated lipid remodeling in microalgae.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced under P at early and late timepoints in DOE1412
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with stronger growth inhibition under P in DOE1412

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phosphate starvation response; resource allocation under P stress
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: photosynthetic genes; cell cycle genes; carbohydrate biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Predicted to downregulate photosynthetic and cell cycle genes and upregulate carbohydrate biosynthesis genes under P
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Modest induction under P is associated with a rewired regulatory program favoring starch accumulation rather than strong canonical phosphate-scavenging output.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cs1228_02g01109 is the PHR1 ortholog in Cs1228 and was only modestly upregulated (~2-fold) under P. 2. Its predicted targets differed markedly from DOE1412, including photosynthetic, cell-cycle, and carbohydrate biosynthesis genes. 3. This rewired circuitry is consistent with increased starch granule accumulation under P in Cs1228.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TRN inference; predicted target analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of phosphate-starvation resource allocation in Cs1228
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite orthology with canonical plant PHR1, its target set appears rewired in Cs1228.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for redirecting carbon allocation between starch and lipid under phosphate stress.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Modestly upregulated under P in Cs1228
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with weaker TAG accumulation and greater starch accumulation under P

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated in TAG-accumulating conditions and proposed as a conserved TAG-related regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cs1228_21g10473 is one of two C3H ortholog pairs highlighted as upregulated in TAG-accumulating conditions. 2. It belongs to the small set of conserved TAG-related TFs shared between strains. 3. Its behavior supports the existence of conserved transcriptional pathways for TAG accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TRN inference; orthology analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate conserved regulator of TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrogen starvation; phosphorus starvation (P t2 high TAG condition)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target genes are not explicitly listed in the text excerpt.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for microalgal engineering because it is conserved and induced in high-TAG states.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated in TAG-accumulating conditions and proposed as a conserved TAG-related regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDOE1412_2079g07848 is the DOE1412 ortholog of Cs1228_21g10473. 2. It was upregulated in TAG-accumulating conditions. 3. It supports a conserved transcriptional pathway contributing to TAG accumulation across strains.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TRN inference; orthology analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate conserved regulator of TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrogen starvation; phosphorus starvation (P t2 high TAG condition)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the conserved C3H-type orthologs emphasized in the abstract and results.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering robust high-TAG responses in DOE1412-like backgrounds.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated under all conditions and classified as TAG-related TF, supporting conserved TAG regulatory circuitry.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cs1228_02g00899 is part of the second conserved C3H ortholog pair. 2. It was upregulated in both species under all conditions according to discussion. 3. It is proposed to participate in conserved TAG-accumulation pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TRN inference; orthology analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate conserved regulator contributing to TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as evidence that some TAG-regulatory pathways are conserved while others are rewired.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate broadly useful regulator for cross-strain TAG engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated broadly and considered part of conserved TAG-related regulatory pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDOE1412_2296g01133 is the DOE1412 ortholog of Cs1228_02g00899. 2. C3H-type orthologs were upregulated in both strains. 3. The authors interpret this as evidence for conserved transcriptional pathways for TAG accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TRN inference; orthology analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate conserved regulator contributing to TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in discussion as one of the conserved C3H-type regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential conserved regulatory target for lipid engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated in N t2 and P t2 in Cs1228, contrasting with its DOE1412 ortholog, indicating rewired TAG regulatory circuitry.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cs1228_04g03113 is the AP2 ortholog in Cs1228 among shared TAG-related TFs. 2. It was upregulated in N t2 and P t2. 3. Its opposite expression pattern relative to the DOE1412 ortholog supports strain-specific rewiring of TAG regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TRN inference; orthology analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent regulator contributing to strain-specific TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contrasts with downregulated DOE1412 AP2 ortholog under TAG-accumulating conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents a switch-like regulator candidate whose manipulation may redirect strain-specific carbon allocation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in N t2 and P t2

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulated in N t2, N t3, and P t2, opposite to the Cs1228 ortholog, consistent with rewired strain-specific regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDOE1412_2160g02163 is the AP2 ortholog in DOE1412. 2. It was downregulated in N t2, N t3, and P t2. 3. This opposite expression relative to the Cs1228 ortholog points to shared but divergently rewired regulatory pathways between strains.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TRN inference; orthology analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent regulator indicating rewired TAG control in DOE1412
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of only three TAG-related TF ortholog groups shared between strains.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for rewiring transcriptional programs toward even greater lipid accumulation or altered timing.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in N t2, N t3, and P t2

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; phosphorus-stress membrane remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: glyoxylate cycle; TAG biosynthesis; DGTS biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Potentially controls citrate synthase, DGAT, and BTA1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely coordinates multiple branches of carbon partitioning and lipid remodeling in Cs1228.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cs1228_04g0256 was highlighted as an NF-YC TAG-related TF with multiple interactions. 2. It potentially controls citrate synthase, DGAT, and BTA1. 3. This suggests a coordinated role in glyoxylate cycle activity, TAG synthesis, and DGTS synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRN inference from FullNet; mutual-information-based interaction analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Multi-pathway regulator integrating carbon flux into storage and membrane lipids
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified from network analysis of rate-limiting steps, not direct wet-lab validation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target because one TF may coordinately reprogram multiple lipid-related pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Classified among TAG-related TFs in Cs1228 under high-TAG conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect several metabolic pathways simultaneously, potentially altering growth/carbon balance.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; biofuel feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: TAG biosynthesis and Calvin cycle coordination
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Potentially controls expression of DGAT- and RuBisCO-encoding genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely links carbon fixation and TAG biosynthesis in high-lipid DOE1412.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDOE1412_2202g05533 was identified as a GATA-family TAG-related TF with multiple predicted targets. 2. It potentially controls DGAT and RuBisCO genes. 3. This indicates involvement in both TAG biosynthesis and Calvin-cycle-linked carbon allocation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRN inference from FullNet; mutual-information-based interaction analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate integrator of carbon fixation shutdown and TAG accumulation in DOE1412
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as an example of TFs controlling more than one metabolic pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for metabolic rewiring to enhance lipid yield while controlling carbon allocation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Classified among TAG-related TFs in DOE1412 under high-TAG conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May impact photosynthesis via RuBisCO regulation while enhancing TAG synthesis.
